label: Khartoum
sub: Sudan's capital; epicenter of the 2023 war outbreak. RSF seized most of the city within weeks; SAF recaptured it in March 2025 after a major counteroffensive.

label: Omdurman
sub: Twin city to Khartoum across the White Nile; heavily contested throughout the war. SAF recaptured RSF-held western Omdurman in January 2025.

label: Bahri (Khartoum North)
sub: Third city of the Khartoum metro area, site of key oil refinery. SAF retook Bahri in January 2025 after months of intense urban fighting.

label: Khartoum International Airport
sub: Seized by RSF in the opening hours of April 15, 2023; became a major RSF stronghold. SAF recaptured as part of March 2025 Khartoum offensive.

label: Presidential Palace
sub: Targeted by RSF on April 15, 2023; became a symbolic battleground. SAF retook the palace complex in March 2025 as a major symbolic victory.

label: Port Sudan
sub: SAF government headquarters; Sudan's primary Red Sea port and de facto wartime capital. Hosts the internationally recognized 'Hope Government' of Gen. Burhan.

label: El Fasher
sub: Capital of North Darfur; last SAF stronghold in Darfur. Fell to RSF in October 2025 after an 18-month siege, triggering mass atrocities described as genocide against Zaghawa and Fur communities.

label: El Geneina
sub: Capital of West Darfur; site of the 2023 Massalit massacres. Between April and November 2023, RSF and allied militias killed an estimated 10,000–15,000 people, primarily Massalit civilians.

label: Nyala
sub: Capital of South Darfur and Sudan's second-largest city. Captured by RSF in October 2023; now hosts the RSF's 'Government of Peace and Unity' alongside Darfur's parallel administration.

label: Wad Madani
sub: Capital of Gezira State; fell to RSF December 19, 2023, displacing 1M+ people. Recaptured by SAF in January 2025 after 13 months of RSF occupation.

label: El Obeid (Al-Ubayyid)
sub: Capital of North Kordofan State; besieged by RSF for nearly two years. SAF broke the siege in February 2025, securing a key central Sudan logistics hub.

label: Kassala
sub: Eastern Sudan city; saw clashes on April 15, 2023, but SAF maintained control. Serves as a staging point for refugee movements to Ethiopia and Eritrea.

label: Atbara
sub: River port city in northern Sudan; SAF logistics hub. Remained under SAF control throughout the conflict, serving as a key supply corridor to the front lines.

label: Dongola
sub: Capital of Northern State; remained firmly under SAF control. Key administrative center and rear logistics base for SAF northern operations.

label: Sennar Dam
sub: Critical hydroelectric and irrigation dam on the Blue Nile. Control of Sennar Dam and the surrounding area was contested between SAF and RSF; damage would threaten agricultural irrigation across central Sudan.

label: Merowe
sub: Northern Sudan city near a key dam; RSF deployed forces here before the war, triggering the final confrontation with SAF. SAF retained Merowe throughout the conflict.

label: Zamzam IDP Camp
sub: North Darfur; home to ~500,000 IDPs. First site of declared famine in August 2024. RSF assaulted the camp in April 2025, killing hundreds to over 1,500 civilians.

label: Abu Shouk IDP Camp
sub: El Fasher, North Darfur; major IDP camp adjacent to the city. Came under RSF attack during the El Fasher siege, destroying water facilities and aid supply lines.

label: Tawila
sub: North Darfur town with significant IDP population; contested between SAF garrison and RSF forces during the El Fasher siege. Site of humanitarian access disputes.

label: Nertiti
sub: North Darfur town; fell to RSF control as part of their Darfur-wide campaign. Site of reported RSF looting and civilian displacement.

label: Zalingei
sub: Capital of Central Darfur State; fell to RSF as part of their comprehensive Darfur offensive. SAF forces were expelled or defected; RSF gained full state capital control.

label: Al Daein
sub: Capital of East Darfur State; under RSF control. Site of reported atrocities against non-Arab communities and a major node in RSF's Darfur supply chain.

label: Ardamata Neighborhood
sub: El Geneina neighborhood; site of the November 8, 2023 massacre where RSF and Janjaweed killed 800–2,000 Massalit civilians. One of the worst single atrocities of the war.

label: Jebel Marra
sub: Mountainous Darfur region historically held by Sudan Liberation Army-AW; remained contested with RSF advances. Jebel Marra's population includes many Fur ethnic community members.

label: Gedaref
sub: Eastern Sudan agricultural city; SAF-controlled. Major reception point for IDPs fleeing Khartoum and Gezira. Faces significant strain on infrastructure from displaced population.

label: Sennar
sub: Capital of Sennar State; saw fighting between SAF and RSF as RSF advanced from Gezira. Site of significant IDP flows from Wad Madani's fall.

label: Port Sudan Displacement Sites
sub: Major IDP hub near Port Sudan receiving hundreds of thousands of Khartoum and Darfur refugees. Overwhelmed local resources; multiple informal settlement areas established.

label: Adre Border Crossing
sub: Sudan-Chad border point near El Geneina; only authorized cross-border humanitarian aid entry point into Darfur. SAF and RSF both sought to control access at Adre.

label: Tuine Refugee Camp (Chad)
sub: Chad-side camp receiving Darfur refugees. Over 600,000 Sudanese refugees fled into Chad's eastern regions; border camps became severely overcrowded by mid-2023.

label: Renk (South Sudan Border)
sub: South Sudan border town receiving Sudanese refugees, primarily from Khartoum and Kordofan. UNHCR reported massive influx; Renk became the primary entry point into South Sudan.

label: SAF General Command HQ
sub: Sudanese Armed Forces headquarters in Khartoum; primary military target in the opening hours of April 15, 2023. SAF defended and ultimately retained this complex.

label: Gezira Agricultural Zone
sub: Sudan's breadbasket; RSF seized this region in late 2023, imposing 'taxes' on farmers and looting harvests. RSF control disrupted food production for millions.

label: Jebel Amer Gold Mines
sub: North Darfur gold mining area; controlled by RSF. One of Sudan's richest artisanal gold sites, generating estimated $860M annually for RSF through Al Junaid corporation.

label: Wadi Halfa
sub: Far northern Sudan near Egyptian border; remained under SAF control. Entry point for refugees crossing into Egypt; Egyptian forces maintained presence in the area.

label: Kadugli
sub: Capital of South Kordofan State; contested between SAF forces and RSF fighters. Site of ongoing battles as of early 2026 as SAF pushes westward from reclaimed central Sudan territories.

label: Dilling
sub: Second-largest city in South Kordofan; under major RSF-SPLM-N assault on March 28-29, 2026. At least 14 civilians killed (5 children), 23 wounded; roads to Habila cut. Sudan Doctors Network warned of El Fasher-scale catastrophe.

label: Khartoum Oil Refinery (Bahri)
sub: Major oil refinery north of Khartoum; retaken by SAF in January 2025. Its recapture restored critical fuel production capacity and was a key strategic objective of the SAF counteroffensive.

label: El Obeid Airport
sub: North Kordofan airport used by SAF as a logistics hub. Remained a key SAF resupply point during the siege of El Obeid city; RSF failed to capture the airfield.

label: Mellit
sub: North Darfur town; fell to RSF during their encirclement of El Fasher. Blocking of Mellit road cut off a key resupply route to the besieged El Fasher garrison.

label: Edd Port
sub: Red Sea port used by SAF for supplies. Secondary logistics entry point alongside Port Sudan for SAF resupply from regional allies including Egypt and Turkey.

label: Damazin
sub: Capital of Blue Nile State near the Roseires Dam. SAF-held strategic city near Ethiopia border. Site of cross-border aid routes for some Sudanese refugees.

label: Halayib Triangle
sub: Disputed territory claimed by both Sudan and Egypt; Egyptian forces present. Egypt's support for the SAF made this border region strategically significant for SAF logistics.

label: El Fasher Teaching Hospital
sub: Only major functioning hospital in North Darfur during the El Fasher siege. Under repeated threat of RSF attacks; WHO documented attempts to attack the medical facility.

label: Gezira IDP Sites
sub: Multiple IDP settlement areas across Gezira State following RSF takeover of December 2023. Over 1 million people displaced; many sought shelter in SAF-held areas to the east.

label: South Khartoum
sub: Southern districts of Khartoum including Jabal Awliya; saw protracted fighting between SAF and RSF. Significant civilian displacement and infrastructure destruction throughout 2023–2024.

label: Halfa El Jadida
sub: Town in River Nile State; remained SAF-controlled throughout the conflict. Part of the northern Sudan corridor connecting Port Sudan to central SAF forces.

label: Kassala Refugee & IDP Zone
sub: Kassala State hosts hundreds of thousands of Sudanese IDPs from Khartoum and Blue Nile, as well as Eritrean refugees. Healthcare and food security severely strained.

label: Tine Border Crossing
sub: South Darfur border crossing to Chad; key route for Sudanese refugees and limited humanitarian aid. Control contested and access restricted by both SAF and RSF at various times.

label: Shendi
sub: River Nile State town; SAF-held logistics and resupply point between Port Sudan and the Khartoum front. Used as a staging area for SAF forces during the capital counteroffensive.

label: Abu Hamad
sub: River Nile State junction town; SAF controlled. Key road and rail junction on the Nile for moving supplies south toward Khartoum.

label: En Nahud
sub: West Kordofan town; came under RSF influence as the conflict expanded from Darfur into Kordofan. RSF used as a supply route connecting Darfur to central Sudan.

label: Bahri Displacement Zone
sub: Khartoum North IDP concentration area; hundreds of thousands fled fighting in Bahri, one of the war's most active urban battle zones through late 2024.

label: Geneina Airport
sub: El Geneina International Airport; site of the June 14, 2023 assassination of West Darfur Governor Khamis Abakar. RSF controlled the airport throughout the West Darfur campaign.

label: South Kordofan Battle Zone
sub: Broad area of South Kordofan contested between SAF and RSF forces as of 2025–2026. SAF pushing westward from reclaimed Khartoum; RSF defending Kordofan corridor linking Darfur.

label: Omdurman Hospital District
sub: Omdurman's hospital district severely impacted by fighting. Multiple facilities damaged or occupied by fighters. MSF and ICRC reported inability to access and operate safely.

label: Nyala Airport
sub: South Darfur's main airport; captured by RSF in October 2023 with the fall of Nyala. Became an RSF logistics hub for supplying Darfur operations.

label: Wadi Salih (Mukjar area)
sub: Central Darfur locality with significant non-Arab population; heavily affected by RSF atrocities and displacement. Entire communities of Fur and Masalit fled the area in 2023.

label: Kassab IDP Camp
sub: North Darfur IDP camp north of El Fasher; received massive influx during the El Fasher siege. RSF attacks on surrounding areas threatened camp access roads.

label: Jabal Awliya Dam IDP Zone
sub: South Khartoum area near Jabal Awliya Dam; became an IDP gathering point as civilians fled fighting in the capital. Overcrowded with limited food and water access.

label: Roseires Dam
sub: Blue Nile State hydroelectric dam; SAF-controlled strategic infrastructure. One of Sudan's key power generators; RSF attacks on power infrastructure threatened dam operations.

label: Abu Zabad
sub: West Kordofan town; RSF seized as part of corridor between Darfur and central Sudan. SAF airstrikes targeted RSF positions in the area.

label: Bara
sub: North Kordofan town; RSF recaptured on March 16, 2026 after earlier SAF seizure. Strategically located north of El Obeid, its recapture tightens RSF encirclement pressure on the SAF-held North Kordofan capital.

label: Kurmuk
sub: Blue Nile State town on the Ethiopia border; captured by RSF-SPLM-N alliance on March 23, 2026. About 73,000 people fled; the fall threatens Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam. Sudan accused Ethiopia of facilitating the assault.

label: Sarf Umra
sub: North Darfur town under RSF control; struck by SAF drones on March 26, 2026 hitting the main market, government HQ, and a fuel tanker with civilian casualties reported.

label: Ad Dabbah
sub: Northern State town on the Nile; struck by RSF drone on March 21, 2026 killing 6 people and damaging the engineering college and electricity substation. Demonstrated RSF's long-range drone reach into deep SAF-controlled territory.

label: Lagawa
sub: West Kordofan town under RSF control; struck twice by SAF drones in March 2026 — March 23 (15 killed) and March 27 (15 killed) — killing 30+ in residential neighborhoods.

label: Ed Damazin
sub: Capital of Blue Nile State near the Roseires Dam; SAF-held but threatened by RSF-SPLM-N advance after fall of Kurmuk on March 23, 2026. Loss would cut power to central and eastern Sudan.

label: Al Sunut
sub: West Kordofan town under RSF control; struck by SAF drones twice in March 2026 — March 10 (50+ killed in lorry strike) and March 27 (7 killed at funeral, 39 wounded). Pattern of repeated drone targeting.

label: Geissan
sub: Blue Nile district ~200 km southeast of Ed Damazin; RSF-SPLM-N military buildup on March 28, 2026 forced 425+ families to flee. Its fall would directly threaten Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam.

label: Al-Rahad
sub: North Kordofan town; hit by SAF drone strike on March 26, 2026 targeting a civilian passenger vehicle, killing 6 and injuring 12. Part of the wave of attacks across Kordofan and Darfur.

label: Dalang
sub: Nuba Mountains city in South Kordofan; site of a major RSF multi-front assault on April 2, 2026 that was repelled by SAF forces. RSF assault cohesion weakened by tribal tensions within recruited units.

label: Al-Jabalain
sub: White Nile State town; RSF drone strikes on April 3, 2026 struck Al-Jabalain Hospital's operating theater and maternity ward during an immunization campaign, killing 10 (including 7 medical staff) and wounding 19. Attack in SAF-controlled territory confirmed RSF long-range drone reach.

label: Rabak
sub: Capital of White Nile State; RSF drone struck a medical supply depot in Rabak on April 3-4, 2026, concurrent with the Al-Jabalain Hospital attack, further degrading healthcare capacity in SAF-controlled White Nile State.

label: Wadi al-Hout
sub: North Kordofan area; SAF repelled a large-scale RSF ground assault on April 6, 2026, preventing RSF from reversing SAF territorial gains in North Kordofan made since the relief of El Obeid and recapture of Bara.

label: Al-Tukma
sub: Strategic area ~7km east of Dilling, South Kordofan; SAF forces seized Al-Tukma from RSF-SPLM-N on April 8, 2026, reopening the Dilling–North Kordofan road and breaking the RSF partial siege of Dilling.

label: Damrat al-Firdous
sub: Central Darfur area under RSF control; site of a SAF drone strike on April 8, 2026 that killed Hamid Ali Abubakar, commander-in-chief of the RSF-aligned Sudanese Awakening Revolutionary Council (RAC) and security adviser to Hemedti, during a command meeting.

label: Umm Dukhun
sub: Central Darfur district under RSF control; struck by drone fire on April 16, 2026 — at least 8 munitions fired, killing 24+ civilians and injuring approximately 20 others.

label: El Fula (Al-Fula)
sub: Capital of West Kordofan State; drone strike hit El Wahda market on April 16, 2026, killing 9 civilians (3 women, 6 children).

label: Kutum
sub: North Darfur town ~100 km northwest of El Fasher; under RSF control. Site of a drone strike on April 10, 2026 that struck a wedding gathering in the Al-Salama neighborhood, killing at least 30 including 17 children and injuring 107. UN Secretary-General condemned the attack.

label: Muqja Station
sub: Blue Nile State railway station between Kurmuk and Ed Damazin. Captured by RSF-SPLM-N after the fall of Kurmuk (March 23, 2026); recaptured by SAF's 4th Infantry Division on April 21, 2026. Controls the supply axis to the Roseires Dam.

label: Sultan Medical Complex (El Geneina)
sub: Medical facility in RSF-controlled El Geneina, West Darfur. Struck by a drone on April 20, 2026, killing 3 people and igniting fires — the latest in a pattern of healthcare attacks across Sudan with 2,052+ killed in 217+ attacks documented by WHO.

label: Um Dukhun
sub: Town in Central Darfur's Buram locality near the Chad border. Struck by SAF drones on April 17, 2026, killing 24+ civilians in market areas including an entire family of six; part of a combined 44-civilian death toll across Darfur and North Kordofan in that single day.

label: Al-Hamidiyah IDP Camp
sub: Displaced persons camp on the outskirts of RSF-controlled Zalingei, Central Darfur. SAF drone strike at dawn on April 27, 2026 killed 6 civilians and injured 15, destroying multiple homes. The camp shelters thousands of displaced women and children who fled fighting across Darfur. Documented by Emergency Lawyers Sudan.
